review_text: Beautiful lawned sites,very clean park, great facilities, will definitely stay here again

Pretty good as far as caravan parks go. I'm not really a fan.of any caravan park but sometimes there aren't other options.
The reason I'm not a fan is the lack of privacy and people (and dogs) can be noisy at times.
Having said that, my powered site was in a more quiet part of the park and thankfully, by 10pm everyone was quiet. It would be good to have a bit of natural screening between sites.
Other than that, the facilities are kept clean and the staff are very friendly.
Also, you can get lucky and strike a special deal.
Oh, and don't be surprised if you come across a few wild non-paying guests 😃

Thank you to Tony and the awesome team at Discovery Parks - Coffin Bay. We stayed in a cabin. Beds super comfortable, shower excellent, superbly located, and just a beautiful place to go. Big thank you 🙏

Great location opposite Oyster HQ. Drive through site we had close to the park area was big and flat. Bathrooms were large with plenty of toilets and showers. Never had to wait to use either. Lots of space to hang washing. Camp kitchen is small.
For the Easter long weekend there were activities planned in the park as well as posted information for community events.
Every day there were emus and kangaroos that passed through opposite our campsite. Was a delight 🦘
Excellent park. Large sites, good location and reasonable rates. Very good amenities. Highly recommend.
It's a good base camp to explore the area , walking 🚶‍♀️ distance to most things,a bit dry & dusty at this time of year & a popular spot 👍we enjoyed our stay in march 2025

Stayed in Match for 10 nights in our caravan.  The location in perfect and the amenities were spotless.  Very sheltered and lots of kangaroos at night , emus wandering around. The only callout I have is the doors to the men’s and ladies amenities close with a bang which is loud at night, a spring on the doors would fix this.

Great park in Coffin Bay
Amenities clean and tidy
Friendly staff
Our site wasn’t the best mainly sand and on a slight slope
Will definitely stay here again but will hopefully get a drive though site next time

Travelling Aussies 🇦🇺

A great caravan park in Coffin Bay with lovely helpful staff 🏆

Plenty of trees and spotlessly clean facilities

Unfortunately, the park wifi is limited and the Telstra mobile service is 4G (March 2026).  Television reception is great with a full suite of channels available

Still a great place to stay ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Good central town setting and well-run clean facilities. However, it's not a great tent camping place. Everyone is shoe-horned in and it feels like a big carpark. Tent sites were a bit of an afterthought as they mostly just clung to the edge of the gravel road or were in a large exposed field. We happened to get one of 3 nice sites along the edge of the tree-line which was great, but I would have been disappointed with what was left had we not got one of those.

Up there with the shabbiest of parks we've encountered travelling Australia, really not aesthetically pleasing whatsoever but it did the job. Very messy looking and just dirt everywhere. You're  essentially camped in dirt, people's old sullige water and whatever poos there at night, there's poo pellets everywhere. grass in terrible shape, sites as unlevel as you can really get. Amenities are ok, they're fine just a little dirty, the dump point good, playground good for kids, just the rest is really shabby looking
Top park, stayed here before, will be back.

We booked an unpowered site online about an hour before arriving, and the lady at reception was still there at 5pm, so check-in was quick and easy. We could choose between two large areas with plenty of space. The sites were clearly and thoughtfully divided so everyone could park properly and respectfully – which we really appreciated. We’ve stayed at other campgrounds with a “choose your spot” system and it can feel chaotic, but here it was very well organized.
We were close to the amenities, which were very clean – even in the morning. Whether it was particularly respectful guests or regular cleaning, it was enjoyable at all times.
We also appreciated the note on the back of the campground map explaining why some areas were less developed and outlining upcoming improvement projects. The transparency and care for guests really stood out. Even with improvements underway, it was clear that guest comfort was being considered.
We visited after peak season, so it was very affordable ($24 per night for an unpowered site) and not too crowded. We would definitely book this campground again if passing through Coffin Bay. A very pleasant stay! 🌿✨
It was a very nice place to stay very friendly and helpful people
Our cabin was clean very comfortable and convienent to local shops and beautiful  Oyster walk. Staff maintain the property extremely well.
My favourite go to Park for a relaxing getaway, love sitting on the deck watching the Kangaroo's, Emu's, Magpie's and Galah's
Great location. Clean amenities. Nice grass area. They often host wine tastings, wood fired pizza in the peak season. Visited 4 times and will be back
Stayed in the caravan park ,great camp kitchen, showers were shit very disappointed

WARNING - Don't stay here if you are tent camping. This place is basically exclusively for caravans and RVs. It is densely packed with these massive vehicles and caravans, everyone with their awning set ups. Surprised they can all open their doors. If you're into that (which evidently a lot ofnepople are, fine). We however were in a car and tent camping, so hired the only available spots - unpowered. The unpowered sites are in a dusty gravel carpark out the back (the front main RV lot faces the coast and has grass and trees). This hellscape at the back at a 2 night minimum at $50 a night. The ground was too hard for any tent pegs. The toilet block was gross and broken. Literally any roadside rest stop would be better than this area. The other unpowered site area was the leftover wedge of (dead) grassed area, totally sloped and not suitable as a site. They've clearly just tried to milk every square metre of land they had. Paid the 2 night minimum but left after 1 night it was so bad. One of the biggest rip-offs I've had in a while.
Wow, a great place to stop over for a few days 😄

First time at coffin bay. My stay here was enjoyable besides all the sinks were blocked in our cabin and the public showers/sinks was also blocked up. We didn’t have a shower in our cabin but can only imagine what that might have been like. Besides that friendly staff and a beautiful spot. Will likely stay here again.

Without a doubt one of the best parks we've stayed in. Van sites are spacious and shady. The view is fabulous. The team who work here are very friendly,  always giving a smile and a wave but also stopping for a chat. All facilities are spotless. Nice range of sauces and jams in reception. The manager Peter is one of the nicest blokes you would want to meet. Very friendly and happy, regularly stopping for a chat. He gets his hands dirty which shows complete respect for his staff. Would stay again in a heartbeat.
Perfect spot opposite outstanding restaurant Oyster HQ. Kangaroos everywhere at night. Good hot showers with great pressure. Easy access sites.

Great little carvan park, very close to shops etc, very clean and great staff.
Very close to and highly recommend the Oyster Walk trails along tge coast.
Fantastic thanks
Very clean and tidy.
Had a one night stay, unpowered site. Very clean and new facilities. Friendly staff, Coffin Bay is an absolute gem. A must visit
Everything you'd expect of a caravan park. The accommodation (Superior Cabin) is getting a bit worn and tired, but as a home base for exploring the lower Eyre, it is perfectly fine.

We unfortunately were not overly happy with our caravan site.
Our site was wet when we arrived as the sprinkler had been on it. Unfortunately creating a mud pit outside where our caravan door would be. Due to availability there was no other sites that could fit our van.
The location is nice and staff are kind but I personally felt boxed in and crowded. Not my fave caravan park in 8 months on the road.

My partner and I stayed at the discovery park in Coffin Bay for 3 days. The cabin we stayed in was well spacious accommodating for 6 people. Great for a family. The staff were very lovely and friendly. The overall view was very relaxing and a very comfortable feel. Many happy people always willing to have a chat or a smile and wave. The park does activities there to do so interaction is amazing. Wood oven pizza and wine taste test, sausage sizzle and many other things at around 5pm at the rotunda. You will wake up to emus and go to sleep with kangaroos. I was lucky enough to make friends with a mumma roo and her joey in her pouch very special moment. The cabin has an amazing bed very comfortable and very clean. If you find something wrong like we did with a power plug not working please tell the staff so they can fix it or any other things you may encounter. They are very effective and effective with maintaining there cabins as well as the park in general. Was an absolute amazing place to unwind for a few days. I really didn't want to leave it was that beautiful
Site was not flat staff very helpful oysters unbelievable
Always great coming here the staff are friendly and funny.
Good spot.  Great sites.  Helpful friendly staff

Stayed in our caravan. Staff were absolutely fantastic. Very friendly and helpful with any questions about the are, food, fishing etc...
Well priced, lots of wildlife in and walking through the park. Great location,  a must for every bucket list. Highly recommended
Nice big park.  Good location. Enjoyed having a fire and the Kangaroos and Emus.

Lovely caravan park with good amenities, very clean cabin with everything you need and even under cover parking.
Would highly recommend and definitely go back again.
Love this park friendly staff clean amenities close to everything big sites . highly recommend this park.
Got a cabin at the park, check in was easy, cabin was clean and had everything you need. Good spot to stop in Coffin Bay
Off grid camping up the back, Great
Lovely park. Close to esplanade. Opposite oyster farms. More for mature people. No playgrounds or jumpy pillows. Emus wonder through park.

A great location and a great park.
Emus and Kangaroos frequent the park grounds and amazed young and old.
Great RV bays with a choice of back-in or drive through. Very friendly staff, clean amenities, bbq and fireplace.

Great location; lovely clean, modern amenities; well kept grounds. Close to Oysters HQ across the road. The local hotel has a courtesy bus too. The Yacht Club up the road has great food. The National Park drive is tar so go out there.

Great cabin stay. Just one night on business but had everything I needed.  Very clean cabin with all the cooking gear and utensils you could possibly need to cook up a feed.
Cabin was very clean and excellent hot water in the shower
Beautiful spot
Quiet at night. Clean park. Pet friendly. Love seeing green lawn/grass. Staff are great too.

One of the most unpleasant and least dog friendly sites I’ve ever been to. We checked in to Coffin Bay Caravan Park 2 days ago after booking online, travelling with 2 adults and a miniature dachshund. Upon arrival, we inquired if there were any cabins available as we’re aware our dog can bark protectively if people walk too near to him. We were advised none of the cabins allow dogs and were given our powered site right near the common use BBQ area.
After setting up, our dog appeared a bit unsettled, particularly when other dogs were barking near him, so we took him for a 2 hour walk to ease him.
Upon returning, the park staff and patrons were using the BBQ area, and with other dogs barking, we put our dog in our swag to prevent him from barking much. Amongst other dogs barking and a couple of dogs fighting near the BBQ area, a member of staff came over and told us we need to make our dog stop barking. We mentioned that he had calmed since being in the swag and we can’t particularly help if he reactively barks when other dogs are barking and fighting. The member of staff told us that’s not her problem and inferred we need to stop him from barking or leave.
Considering this was at 6pm, this left us in the position of packing up our equipment and leaving for another campsite when it had already started to become dark with the office already being closed, preventing us from inquiring about a refund considering we used the site for all of 3 hours.
Great amenities, unfriendly and rude staff that lack understanding caused major issues with our camping holiday.
Other dogs continued to bark as we left the site. We will definitely not even consider staying here again or recommending this site to others.
Very cold and windy.
Great sites for big rigs, friendly staff, clean amenities.

A great park with spacious sites between cabins and for vans.  Neat and tidy. One issue we had was immediately addressed and remedied the same morning. A lovely spot to explore the Coffin Bay National Park.

This is the most beautiful park in South Australia.  You are right next to the National Park and your neighbours are emus, kangaroos and countless birds.  The staff are very friendly and helpful and they maintain the park in excellent condition.  If you don't have your own accommodation, the cabins are well appointed, clean and comfortable
Great park with spacious drive through van sites.
No complaints from me. Relaxing.

Great caravan Park & first class amenities.....best ever van parking plan & clean facilities.
This caravan Park sets the standard that others should follow.
Highly recommended for short or longer term stays.

Wonderful park with a great winter price for G'day Members, fabulous location right opposite Oyster HQ & nearby Coffin Bay Cafe, easy access to Oyster Walk along the foreshore. Wine tastings twice a week, fantastic Pizza Van on Sundays, communal fire every night - fantastic to have these activities still going through off season. And I loved that the Location Map didn't have any rules on the back - treat your visitors with respect & 99% will act accordingly. I will be back, often.
Loved this place.. friendly staff,  clean amenities and best of all a community camp fire 🔥, already lit and waiting for us🍷🍻
Excellent location,  park is spotless,  has all the amenities,  will come again.

Great park to stay in.
Awesome location.  Within walking distance to middy places.
More clothes dryers would be helpful.
Great park very relaxing and the best oysters across the road

Could not find a better park than this one,
We stayed a few days in a cabin and it was too short,
Staff are super friendly and helpful,
Cabin was super clean and spacious with everything we could need supplied,
The park is so well kept and wildlife wonder around daily,

Had an amazing caravan site backed onto the beach.
The sunrise and sunsets were outstanding and the dolphins put on a show every night.
Beautiful place

Wonderful location. Across the road from the bay. 5 minutes to the Oyster Tour and HQ Oyster Restaurant. The Trail walk starts from the edge of the park which takes you along the waters edge. Nature and wild life is great. Emus wondering through the park and around Coffin Bay Township.

Great place best caravan park I've stayed in, so peaceful and close to everything you need.
I'd like to thank the couple in site 76 that found my phone on the morning we were checking out cheers 🍻
Beautiful spot good facilities

Great location, management and staff were really friendly.
We were on a powered site with our little dog.
We attended an open all welcome charity sausage sizzle and oyster shucking demo.
Was a nice social evening.
The facilities are excellent, very clean amenities including plenty of washing machines and lines for drying. Dryers as well if needed.
My favourite park so far on this trip.  amenities spotless park spotless matting provided on site.  emus wander around park.  staff lovely.  highly recommend

Beautiful location with kangaroos and emus just relaxing in the park...the welcome I received when checked in was so nice and  sincere so well done girls.  We loved the cabin, so clean, bright and perfect location so we cld walk everywhere...definitely will stay again so thankyou for making our holiday so special
Right across the road from Oyster HQ, easy access to NP and pub not far. Amenities were very clean and modern, price in low season very reasonable

Coffin Bay is just fantastic and was able to get this cabin at Discovery Parks for such a great price. The grounds are lovely, the staff were friendly. Had a wonderful time and am planning to be back there this year.

Wow what an awesome park we have been to quite a few in the 3 months we have been travelling & this 1 by far out dose them all in area price service after beeing on the road for so long it is nice to come to a park that just care
Good location .
Friendly staff and great location.

The reception at Coffin Bay Discovery Park is exceptional, the office and grounds staff are very friendly and helpful. The site we were allocated for our caravan was terrible. Yes, the site may have been long enough to fit our van, but, parks need to also consider manoeuvrability to get the caravan on the site, and then the drop off from road level to the site, what a joke, ended up doing minor damage to our rear bumper because of the awkward angle we were on. Please, please, parks, consider more than the size of the site when making your allocations. The sites on the fence, 61 & 60 are woeful to put a larger van on. Seek advice from a person who is experienced in towing and reversing a caravan, because not all sites are suitable for all caravans. So many who work in caravan offices have no idea what the sites in their park even look like. Get out of the office and get familiar with your park so you can provide a more pleasurable experience for your guests. Also, if there is a mat on the site, clean it between guests. If you're getting a lot of traffic through you're making more money, pay someone to give the mats a blow or sweep off please. Ours was disgusting.
On a positive note the amenities were impeccable. Beautifully cleaned and maintained.

Nice place just not level in some parts. Again clean and tidy facilities
If you like oysters lot's available

We stayed at the Caravan Park and location was fine but the caravan site they gave us was Not Very good at all especially for a caravan. The one's near the Office. Make sure you book a Drive through site . I should have said something but I'm not like that. I think it was 19
Friendly staff, great location and facilities
Great park, fire pit every nite, drive though  sites, clean amenities walking distance  to IGA and takeaway

We stayed here site #55 with our 2 pups.|Our site was on a slope.|Great position within easy walk of most conveniences and the Oyster Walk ways.|The park has a designated Pet gear washing machine, which was nice,at $4/load.|The toilets are clean.|Showers have mould, could do with some attention. |We enjoyed our time here in Coffin Bay.

Well done Coffin Bay! It was a pleasure to stay 5 nights on a powered site.
The booking process was efficient & friendly. The site was well set up. Your sausage sizzle was a lovely evening. It was a pleasant surprise to find everything in the park so well maintained. Thank you.
Very busy everyone friendly

A level site is a make or break for some van/motorhome owners.
Ours was the break. No matter how we positioned our motorhome we could not get it straight, so out came all the blocks & chocks we had to make it as even as we could.
We were put on an oval shaped piece of land with other vans all higgledy-piggledy, drainage hoses all over the place.
After speaking to other van owners apparently they were asked if they had brought chocks or blocks with them, so clearly the owners/managers knew there was a problem on that section of the park.
Being a pet friendly park, dogs barking was a problem.
Amenities were clean.
Amenities needed updating
Only the one night, but another 2 would have been better!!

Stayed here for 4 nights..it was not value for money.and not home away from home..4 years ago we were here and the difference in service and amenities has really taken a dive.|For instance we had 1 and a half sachets of dishwashing liquid..yes we had someone's left overs...the kitchen lacking basics when it comes to using the oven..no oven trays to cook in...||The beds were bunks and a double....the bedding we were given was 1 very thin blanket and a sheet ..we froze the 1st night.||We will not stay here again and would not recommend this to anyone else....on checking|The local rentals they are cheaper than what we paid for substandardconditions

Was a good stay in powered site but a massive negative was the lack of sullage drain points, we couldn't shower or do our washing in our own caravan due to the water run off goes straight over the site next to us. Some of these are new sites but  they didn't go to that next step of doing grey water

Excellent clamping tents - close to all amenities. Spotless room with everything you could ask for. Also a short walk to Oyster HQ and the Yacht Club Spent two nights there, will return again. We also walked along the Oyster Walk from end to end
ANSWER YOUR BLOODY PHONE
Great park close to everything.
Nice and tidy park with plenty of sites and cabins management are excellent sausage sizzle on Thursday nights

It's a great spot to explore coffin bay from.
Hq oysters just over the road 🤩

5 stars for the woman from reception! She was the only one in town that wanted to help me when my phone stopped working and I needed wifi only to use my laptop to figure out how to get my phone working again. Thank you so much! You're the hero of the day for me ❤️
Lovely park, good water, great amenities. But dusty right now but that's most of SA at the moment.

Booked 3 months in advance and given the worst site. Was far from flat. Usually takes us 20 minutes to set up. After 2 hours, still couldn't find a flat spot and took a long time to TRY and level van. We looked around and ours definitely worst site. Why?

A great caravan park in lovely location near the ocean. We had a powered site with some much needed shade during the hot weather. Quiet most times with very clean and convenient amenities.
Such a shame that our arrival was soured by the rude woman at Reception who didn't offer a greeting , just a one word question " Name ? " and then she proceeded to get us out the door as quickly as possible even though no other customers were in there. A quality caravan park like this one deserves better PR.

Interesting check-in, requested a late checkout (part of my membership) and was told that others start checking in from 11:00 (normal check-in is 14:00) and it would inconvenience them if they have to wait. Normally I wouldn’t complain but literally being made like your stay isn’t valued didn’t
Take me enjoy my stay here. Toilets were dirty and showers were grimy in the small ablution.
A nice spot, right near the water. Too many noisy galahs (the bird kind), but not much they can do about that. All up, it was a good spot to stay.
The park had an overall great feel to it and was just across the road from the beautiful bay

Located just behind Oyster HQ and next to a well stocked convenience store.  Small supermarket a few hundred metres south
We had drive through site 99, allowing you to park your vehicle in front id your van.  A nylon mat secured to the ground for your annex.  Clean amenities, camp kitchen and BBQs
Hand soap at the dump point!
Would recommend
Don’t miss the experience of Oyster HQ and its oyster vending machine!

Great location, would have given five stars for everything and we know it is a small thing, the hair  conditioner pump did not work, and it was locked in so we could not access another way. Cheers, Tania and Roy
Receptionist wasn’t very friendly. Place is close to Oyster Farm , easy access of amenities in the place. Close to National Park . Area is located near a beach .

Coffin Bay is a lovely quiet little oyster town, after 9pm you can hear a pin drop. My rating is a little biased because of the dry conditions, dusty and windy most days which make it unpleasant. We have been here during winter and it was very green and no wind most days  unlike summer.
The van park has great amenities block, clean and pleasant showers and toilets, nice camp kitchen, laundry, and a rotunda / meeting area for social interaction.
Do check out the golf / sports club and the yacht club and if you want a great deal on oysters take a drive around the oyster sheds and see if any one is working, a few shell direct from the lease at great prices.
Very nice park close to everything
Great staff, clean park. Nice to go for a walk down to the Jetty and nice restaurants close by. Definitely be back!
We paid $300 per night (4 kids 2 adults) and couldn’t even get hot water for the dishes. Grumpy receptionist and disgusting amenities location is great park is not

Great caravan park. Very friendly and accommodating staff. Amenities cleaned regularly throughout the day. We stayed in the unpowered section which was full of native birds and wallabies as well as had some nice shaded sites.

We had a family Christmas in two caravans and a swag. Plenty of room on our drive through sites and close to all attractions. The park had plenty of activities for the kids to join in as well as oysters and sausage sizzle with wine tasting on Boxing Day. Very clean amenities and the staff went above and beyond to make it a special experience.
Extraordinary

We had a wonderful time here the bbq for a donation chance to buy wine from a local supplier and how can you forget oysters.
The guys here make you feel welcome from the time you check in.
We came for 6 days and stayed 6 months. Emus and kangaroos happily coexist in Coffin Bay

If I could give it 6 stars I would.
Facilities are excellent, staff are very friendly  and gave us discount vouchers for local businesses in town upon arrival.
Location is walking distance to pretty much everything.

As a lot of other reviews, the problems are with the sites. Unlevel / uneven is an understatement. I've read they are waiting for the quiet period to address the problem, we stayed in the quiet period, no activity.||The rest of the park deserves 4 stars. Amenities are top notch and beyond clean.||No need to return
I would have given it a 5 star except for the emu's that shat everywhere. But it was a great park

Super freundlich, sauber, ordentliche Sanitäranlagen.

Nachts streifen Kängurus und Emus durch die Anlage, ein schöner idyllischer Ort zum verweilen. Wir sind auch spontan länger geblieben. Vielen Dank für die vielen Tipps zu Aktivitäten in der Umgebung 🤙

Sven and coworker helped enormously when we arrived without a booking. Managed to slot us into a water view powered site. Very friendly and Sven was so excited about showing us where the best things to see and do and eat at were located. Bathrooms and laundry were very clean at all times. . Highly recommend. Oh and rates were very reasonable.

Wow! I was really pleased to see how well kept the caravan park is maintained and cleaned. There was literally not one spot of dirt in the ladies bathrooms, all really well cleaned and looked after. Hubby said the mens were the same.  The laundry has heaps of machines available and they even sell the liquid needed for washing. The staff accommodated a last minute booking for us (powered site) and were very welcoming upon arrival. Beautiful location and definitely would recommend. Thank you so much guys!

What a delightful space this is. The staff (all of them) were so professional while also friendly, welcoming and helpful. We only stayed one night but could have easily stayed a week. Price was great. Location handy to everything. Beautiful scenery and visits from the local kangaroos and emus made the space delightful.
All amenities were in excellent condition and clean. Laundry prices were some of the best we have seen.
Dogs are more than welcome on a leash.
Only two minor things - it was a little bit complicated getting into our site as we had a big van and everyone around us was “in place” so manoeuvres were tight. Also, we were parked down by the road so we weren’t able to pick up wifi. But it was not an issue.
Sven & Paul are extremely hospitable hosts. We received the most information at checkin thus far. Very clean & lots of upgrade plans over next 2 years.
I really enjoyed my stay here,
Took to long to wash and dry cloths as limit machines

Lovely holiday park! Super clean amenities, large flat camp spots for unpowered sites. Great little camp kitchen and lots of Emu's which are super cool to see. Great central location in the town and reception very helpful and attentive ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

We had a lovely stay on an unpowered site with water views. The staff were so friendly and helpful and gave us lots of local knowledge when we arrived. |The local emus and kangaroos are an added bounds and had some of the tastiest local oysters. Great park, with clean facilities. Would definitely stay again.
Beautiful park. They have ongoing works to improve sites and facilities. Staff were very helpful and promoted their area. Kangaroos and emus wonder around without a care.
Polished and professional park..emu's wandering around included

Coffin Bay is our favourite Eyre Peninsula destination and the discovery park has such a great location. When we camped on a unpowered site it was so peaceful and we saw kangaroos nearby. Then staying in a cabin, it is very clean and with beautiful views to wake up to. And Sven and the team are super helpful to guide you to the best spots for tourism. Highly recommend 👍
Great service at check in, cabin newly renovated, clean and comfortable. Walking distance to services. We ate at oyster hq and 1802 both wonderful experiences. Great park to stay at.

Probably one of the best places I've stayed. Friendly staff good prices. I can't stop telling my family and friends about it . Also coffin bay is now my favourite place I'm thinking about buying property there.

Fantastic location close to everything in town and the wonderful Oyster Walking Track that takes you along the waterfront and beyond.
Great service and facilities with great grass sites and the wildlife is great to see with emus and kangaroos regular visitors.
Lovely park. Regular visits from kangaroos and emu. Nice facilities and large drive thru   sites
One of the best parks that we have stayed in
I stayed at an unpowered site backing onto the bush. It was just me and the baby roos. Toilet and shower were spotless. Highly recommend.
Fantastic place to stay, communal fire pit is a great idea and the caretakers couldn’t do enough for us. Great location and a short stroll to everywhere. Highly recommended 🤗

We had a great time at this park.  Very welcoming with a get to know you fire pit on the first night and sausage sizzle the second.  Also received a discount voucher for the HQ Oyster restaurant across the road. Yum.
We also had some lovely natives as visitors.

Loved our stay there
Will be staying again
Lovely place to stop, especially if you like oysters.
Stayed in tent site. Great campsite all amenities clean, tidy and they have everything you could need. The emus and kangaroos wandering around were a bonus.

What is there not to like here! Gorgeous park with emus wandering through, wild birds. Nice trees and grass! Amenities clean and awesome showers. Amazing, helpful and friendly staff. Across road from beach and we could see the sea from our site. To top it off an awesome oyster bar across the street

We had a camper and a powered site.
The showers are great and roomy.
Staff was very helpful and knowledgeable about the area and happy to tell you about things you might be interested in.

Caravan Owner - we were on a site in the north eastern side and it had plenty of space for our (quite large) van. Other spots looked a bit cramped, but there were a lot of positives.

1) dedicated washing machine for pet stuff (rare)
2) gave us treats for our dogs when we arrived
3) lots of kangaroo's at night
4) often emu's during the day

I don't entirely remember the washer/dryer situation. I hate dryers that are $4-5 per cycle with no way of extending it and I don't remember hating these dryers. I don't mind if they're $4 for 40 minutes, then you can add $1 for 10 minutes or whichever.

We don't have children with us so I can't comment on the kid-friendly amenities, but all up I felt it earned a very rare 5-star from me for being suited to us (a couple with a pair of dogs and no kids) in a very nice location.

What a beautiful place! We stayed 2 nights.
From the moment I walked in the office Sven was all over my booking, he took the time to explain everything in the area and around the camp ground. Sven is an asset to Coffin Bay Caravan Park.
Our dog was given dog treats on arrival too!
Bathrooms were beautiful and clean. Water pressure in the showers was amazing.
Emu's and kangaroos roam the park, it's just beautiful to watch.

Thank you for a wonderful stay.

Wow what an amazing place to stay! From the moment we arrived the service was excellent, they really cared about how we were feeling and tried to make our stay as pleasant as possible! Very clean facilities, big open fire at night to warm up too. Beautiful camp sites and you can look forward the kangaroos and emus coming to visit you but don’t feed them.

They say “Have a Break, Have a Kit Kat”  and we were welcomed with one on arrival,  perfect after a long drive. So many great things about this place,  ideal  location to stop on a long road trip, especially if you love oysters. Really friendly and helpful greeting on arrival with all the information you need printed for you, the grounds on the way to our cabin had kangaroos grazing,  the cabin is cozy, clean, well appointed with kitchenette and all the appliances you need.  I would highly recommend this place to my fellow travellers.

From arriving and departure we were very happy! The receptionist was super friendly and informative, it was perfectly located across from Oyster HQ where we walked to for dinner. The kangaroos and emus visit the park regularly, we can wait to come back to explore more!

What a friendly reception we received! We were able to book in early and they offered to move us next to our parents who are travelling with us. Lovely drive thru sites. We are close to toilets. Park is a quick walk to Oyster HQ. We received information about the surrounding area. Super helpful would definitely recommend.

We got there to be frustratingly placed on an angled and sloped site which after a full days driving took considerable frustrating time to set up given extra work needed to unhitch and level a large van. Lucky we had wood and levels to fix this. For a park this size would have expected better and flatter sites. Was not a good start to our stay. Worse is your brochure points this out so you know about this but proceed to put vans on the sites anyway!!!. No views to see on the sites so nothing to brag about.  Whilst sites were large being sloped we were unable to use drain pit to dispose of grey water - Neither were any of our neighbours. Van sites at back had to endure water run off onto their sites - would be easy to move current pits to rear of those sloped sites rather than current unusable positions. Not a nice experience for sites at rear of us or our neighbours to have grey water running across their paid sites!!!! Park is dog friendly which is a plus. Walk to local shops and cafe easy. Toilets/showers clean but dated. Dump point was clogged/overflowing. For price paid would have expected better experience. 3 would be best we could give for our experience this time.
Fantastic park located close to the bay and town facilities great amentaties large sites within walking distance if most sites to see in Coffin Bay

We loved the park the staff were so helpful and  nothing was too much trouble. I loved that there was no keypads for amenities, or driving in . The amenities were spotless. The communal Fire pit was great for people to catch up. It was right across the road from the beach and short walk to shops . Thankyou for an enjoyable stay ! 🙏
We had a terrific three night stay. The staff are cheerful and wonderfully agreeable, the spaces are quite large and the new amenities block is terrific.
Some sites a little challenging to reverse into because of the gradience of the site
Parts of park need landscaping. Where we were was not good

Coffin Bay caravan park provided an exceptional stay, combining welcoming staff and great facilities. From the best check-in I have received to the well-maintained grounds and good amenities, the park catered to all our needs, offering a spacious caravan spot.    The park environment  is a perfect blend of comfort and relaxation  making it a delightful park for any camper.  Fantastic value .  A very big thank you to Sandi (the best customer service ever , you went above and beyond 10 stars) and Sven.  Many thanks guys. You rock. We will be back again and again. Ian Sandy
Love the place. Great park. Good size drive through site. Amenities very clean staff very nice
We had an enjoyable stay here in our caravan. We wish we had stayed longer. A very good place to base yourself to do some drives or walks or just eat oysters.

The amenities are the best l have seen in my travels.
But, like all bathrooms, l have seen in my travels.
The exhaust fans are never clean.
Average, sites semi level, reception was rude but second time around met a lovely lady.
Lovely place.
Wonderful people who give you heaps on information. Lovely park
Good clean caravan park, amenities are large and clean, but only 1 to service a large area. Only draw back was people not controlling their dogs, not on a leash and chasing the Emus.
Only stayed on drive through site, clean and tidy othetwise
Good park, very friendly staff, marked down because showers were stone cold for the duration of our stay (24 hrs)
Ok, as far as Caravan Parks go. Good location, but amenities don't include a swimming pool, which is a bit of a disappointment on days of 38+ degs.
Lovely park and well looked after.  LOTS of wildlife around to see

I would give this park 1 out of 10 stars now it used to be a nice green park when the previous owners had it now since Discovery has taken it over it is a dry dust bowl you may as well be camping in the desert do a day visit to Coffin Bay and stay in Port Lincoln instead we even had to dig holes to drop our motor home wheels in to get it levell unless this park changes I won't go there again even around the play ground area the lawn doesn't get watered vehicles can't drive in this area so no excuse for it not to be watered
Very clean park, amenities clean and well maintained. Very friendly staff
In 2 years being on the road full time I must say the hot water and pressure in the showers is the worst I have experienced, saying that the park is very good close to everything and very tidy
Pretty great caravan park. Dog friendly.
I did not have a cabin as we had our caravan but amenities were clean and facilities were generous. Close to walk to oyster HQ and others.

The park has loads of sites however there is no concrete pads and it's a bit undulating so you might have to use levelers.  Close to the waterfront, IGA and oyster experience tours and restaurants etc. With the shark situation a pool would have been great. Staff were very friendly and helpful.

Helpful staff, clean amenities, safe feeling area, close to all the main shops and restaurants in town.

Unpowered site cost about $40 which is a bit steep for a dirt patch in my opinion. (esp compared to other discovery parks that charge half that for better sites with newer facilities). But still a good place for a night with a hot shower and laundry facilities.

Great camping ground. Bathrooms, laundry and camp kitchen were very clean and well maintained. There was a problem with the showers when we first arrived so they were cold but that was fixed in a couple of days so all good.

Don't stay here if you want a warm shower. They purposely turn the hot water off at ALL times. Even showers early in the morning or early evening are freezing. They turned it back on after lots of complaints but only for a few hours. What a horrible and cruel thing to do to families at Christmas time, especially when you are charging people peak period prices. I will not be staying here again. What a disgusting way to save money. Charge a dollar or something at least to use hot water  or use timer showers...but purposely keeping the hot water off all the times is inhumane and just plain rotten. The owners should be ashamed. Talked with a bunch of ladies in the bathrooms about this and we will all never be staying again. Thanks for nothing.
Awesome in every aspect. Clean, organised, perfect spot.

Fantastic pet friendly caravan park with exceptional facilities and amenities. I was grateful for the dedicated pet bedding washing machine, and pleasantly surprised to find two dryers available. Life saver! In beautiful surrounds with a lovely foreshore to explore this is a brilliant place to stay. Highly recommend!

Angry and unkind staff.

I haven't got a confirmation email because I put the wrong email on booking. I rang the staff today (7/12/23) to double-check and the lady who was on the phone was bloody unkind and aggressive. I rang her again as I still haven't received the email after the first phone call just wanted to ask and double check again with her but she seems to be annoyed saying "So what do you wanna know?" being quite aggressive. You shouldn't work in customer service if you treat people depending on your mood. I will make an official complaint to the head office.
Great spot.
Great place quiet and relaxing

We just had 5 days at this park…great park. Amenities blocks and camp kitchen were super clean. Lots of wildlife, kangaroos, emus & birds.
Great park close to shops and jetties. We really enjoyed our stay ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Very friendly, helpful staff and excellent service with setting up our annex.  Great walking trails with spectacular views.
This caravan park is fantastic. Walking distance to shops, restaurants and the water. Plus, this is an oyster lover paradise.
Beaut location. Quiet and peaceful park which is neat and inviting. 4 baby emus strolling through with their parent emu just makes it even better as a great place to stay.
Great park ,very clean. Lots to see nearby and shops walking distance. The best ousters across road at HQ.

By the time we got here we had been in the road for a few weeks. So we’re glad a real bed and the space to stretch. The bed was comfortable and the cabin was clean. Its location to everything in coffin bay was excellent. Coffin Bay has some awesome walking trails.
Lovely place with kangaroos and emus wandering through the park. Would be perfect with concrete pads or even matting.
Amazing.  Only one word. Staff. Location. So idyllic  and the views don't get me started...
Beautiful   parks, great friendly  staff.
Lovely spacious caravan park.  Nice hot showers and clean amenities.  Grass and concrete sites.  Sausage sizzles on a Monday.  Close to the shops and Oyster HQ.

I stay on my own in a caravan. I get a good deal for a week stay. All the people associated to this park were wonderful. Yes there is some emu poo around but they work hard removing it every day and I found no problem so long as I keep my eyes open. Surprisingly few flies about here. Beach is across the road and the IGA store up the road 200 meters or so. The amenities are the best I ever used. Awesome camp kitchen. The showers are large with a hand rail which was a big help for me. And.... the curtain goes almost to the floor... Yehaaaa! Also kudos to the designers who added a 20mm lip on the shower floor to prevent water welling up where you stand to dry yourself. Other parks should take a leaf from this park. I rate this park very high, and do so because of the price. It is a 5 star park for a 4 star price...so once they raise the price, it would then be same as the rest good parks which may cost you patronage. Well done to all associated with this caravan park.
Excellent park, large sites very clean and modern amenities, friendly staff and easy walk to shops and oyster bar. Highly recommend.

Beautiful park managers, very accommodating. Enjoyed our stay in the non powered area. Rest rooms clean and tidy. Unfortunately we had to clean up emu poo piles before we could set up camp, ongoing issue for the park as they roam along with kangaroos.
Good stay nice clean amenities weather not to bad fishing a little slow but still good
Lovely drive through sites, clean amenities blocks. Very close to the National Park entrance. Excellent oysters across the street. Lots of emus, watch out for their Pooh!
Great location, tidy park with clean amenities
Nice park.  Sites not all level. Wildlife excrement a bit of a problem in the park. Receptionist very accommodating. Would stay again.
Great spot & friendly service
Just perfect 🥰

Emus and kangaroos roaming free, birds galore, what a unique caravan park. We opted for an unpowered site and at $23 we thought it might have been a mistake. But no, they charge even less than some showgrounds with much fewer facilities. Lovely staff, and the offer of the Oysters happy hour across at Oysters HQ was a real bonus. Loved our stay.
Facilities are fine. Surrounded by national parks. Emus and kangaroos wander freely throughout the town and caravan park.
Lovely spot to visit. We enjoyed the wildlife, beautiful walks and fine dining. Drive through sites were great. Follow us Sweet-Az RV'n on youtube
Fabulously clean amenities that have been thoughtfully designed. Loved the wildlife casually wandering around. Great location.

The park fees are great at the moment, but due for increase when improvements have been completed re standards for Discovery Parks. Love the wild life having free reign in the park - kangaroos and emus a aplenty ❤

What a beautiful place to stay! Sandi was so welcoming and went above and beyond to tell me what I should do while staying in this beautiful place. Amenities were spotless. And then there’s the emus and kangaroos that just walk/hop around the campground. Spectacular!
Very good amenities and camp kitchen.

Our premium van site was among the road edge opposite the Bay and Oyster HQ (popped over for a feed).  Loved the roos and emus roaming the park.  The park and it's amenities were very clean and good. Only stayed the 1 night and enjoyed the stay.

Great park for a couple of days. Stop over great staff, very helpful ... could do with a bigger kids playground and some other kids activity  ... but great for seeing emus and kangaroos right next to your site ...

Stayed for 2 nights on a powered caravan site and wish I could have stayed 5 nights, the park is clean, close to the shops and coffinbayoystersHQ cafe restaurant is across the road. the amenities are super clean and the staff are bright bubbly friendly who make you feel at home. 10/10 for this one. and dont forget the worlds first Oyster vending machine is just across the road and the oysters are fresh and oh so Yum.

Great park, terrific location, caravan sites well laid out,with grassed sites and also drive through sites, Amenities are great and very clean. Kangaroos and Emu's wonder around. Office staff very friendly and helpful 😀 the park is close to town and the oysters are awesome.
Drive thru sites, gravel and grassed, great size.  Amenities super clean, impressive camp kitchen, laundry has heaps of washers and dryers.  Office staff very efficient. Thank you.

Really enjoyed our stay at Coffin Bay.  Unfortunately the weather wasn't the greatest however the caravan park  was very nice.  The amenities were very good and the drier worked really well (unlike some other places where it took ages to dry clothes).  The Park is located directly opposite the Oyster HQ where a dozen fresh oysters can be purchased anytime from a 'dispenser' for $18.00.   There are good shops in close walking distance and most seem to sell oysters!!!.  We booked the 'cheapest' power site and in retrospect I should have purchased a more expensive site to ensure i got a site that was level.   Staff were friendly and helpful.  Very good camp kitchen.  Emus and Kangaroo's roam the park.
rude staff.
